title: Add Images Directly from File Columns into a PDF Document

posts.author: DocCreate (FlowTech Apps)
posts.cookedHtml:
Unlock the Power of Embedded Images with DocCreate

We’re excited to announce the release of our latest instructional video tutorial, now available on YouTube! This guide walks you step-by-step through one of DocCreate’s most powerful and exclusive features:

Embedding images directly from your board’s file column into your PDF documents.

This groundbreaking feature is unique to DocCreate among all document generation apps on the monday.com marketplace—and it’s a game-changer.

Why it matters:
Whether you’re completing site inspections, photo reports, or form submissions, you can now seamlessly integrate images into your generated documents—no need to manually upload or reformat files.

Combine this with DocCreate’s core functionality—automated, branded document generation—and you’ve got a complete solution for transforming board data into powerful, professional reports.

Watch now to see just how easy it is to bring your documents to life:
https://www.youtube.com/@DocCreate

Need help? Have questions? Reach out to us at support@doccreate.io or visit DocCreate to book a support session.

Let DocCreate do the heavy lifting—generate, automate, and now visualize.

posts.author: JenefaJ (Jenefa)
posts.cookedHtml:
Yes, this is possible with The PDF Maker (https://app.thepdfmaker.com).

The PDF Maker also supports embedding images directly from your board’s file column into your generated PDFs — perfect for use cases like site inspections, client reports, or form submissions. Just add an image placeholder to your PDF template and map it to the file column of your choice.

This allows you to automate rich, professional-looking documents without needing to manually upload or reformat images. Every time a new record is added or updated, the PDF is automatically generated with the image and other relevant data included.

Learn more about setting it up in this support guide.
